name: The White House
subject: Introducing the RAISE Act
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ff97cb9f2efe519128a627dd08d6938ccb1008bfd20ee8ae625c5e597e855efa742e3c3d7f855a6341a85922d6cff8237d536c9f281b88ec

As part of American Dream Week, President Donald J. Trump will introduce the RAISE Act, which will address our current outdated immigration system which fails to meet the diverse needs of our economy.

-------------

President Trump, Senator Cotton, and Senator Perdue Introduce the RAISE Act

Today, Senators Tom Cotton and David Perdue will join President Donald Trump at the White House to introduce The Reforming American Immigration for a Strong Economy Act (RAISE Act). The RAISE Act is aimed at creating a skills-based immigration system that will make America more competitive, raise wages for American workers, and create jobs. Americans deserve a raise.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ff97cb9f2efe5191f147db278f026fac73daf610fd25ab5e1acc9bcd0d0370c364154c34d82ecdbc39724ea665b10c0eeb824dbf358fc7b5
Watch live at 11:30am EST

President Trump Celebrates Small Businesses, “The Engine of the American Dream”

Yesterday, the White House hosted small business owners from around the country, “The Engine of the American Dream.” President Trump delivered remarks, and then SBA Administrator Linda McMahon and Advisor Ivanka Trump held a question and answer session with the attendees, discussing tax reform, growth resources, and regulatory relief.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ff97cb9f2efe519196ef654896f1076397079fb6d96ba22950e9d1ce12091aab49992cd5df5920d57cb1b15928d9e69d3f2c64c3512fad3d
Watch the video

Vice President Pence Delivers Remarks to Noble Partner Participants in Georgia

Yesterday in Georgia, Vice President Pence addressed American and Georgian troops, saying, “The strategic partnership between the United States and Georgia is stronger now than ever before, and this joint exercise is a tangible sign of our commitment to each other to make it stronger still.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ff97cb9f2efe5191957794fe9b79e57b2eab35e2f3c551c3f04a277d6b7c19b6da4b3b4a9df0659c89ef9611b3c3288dba648adb9264fa11
Watch the video

Photo of the Day

Advisor Ivanka Trump and Administrator Linda McMahon | August 1, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS and VP TODAY

Today, President Trump will make an announcement with Senator Tom Cotton and Senator David Perdue from the Roosevelt Room.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ff97cb9f2efe5191f147db278f026fac73daf610fd25ab5e1acc9bcd0d0370c364154c34d82ecdbc39724ea665b10c0eeb824dbf358fc7b5
Watch live at 11:30am EST. The Vice President is in Montenegro, the final stop in his trip abroad strengthening American partnerships. He
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ff97cb9f2efe51918b3ba000e240e38f7acc4af276bdb7a14362784aa84f328669f90bce4029e8b72b0b0d641bdc5dbdea0294b09b75a0d1
delivered remarks at the Adriatic Charter Summit this morning.

Coming Up This Week

Tomorrow, the President will participate in a Veterans Affairs Telehealth event at the White House. On Friday, he will head to FEMA Headquarters for a Hurricane Briefing.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ff97cb9f2efe5191e2962696912528f03dc7147670c8955b8683ef8f20c774f546b9e045a173f30ecadc47c62fee45e147e68921cec2cd22

name: Republican Party of New Hampshire
subject: NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester Statement on New Hampshire Hospital Contract
body:
PRESS RELEASE

For Immediate Release: August 2, 2017

Contact: Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310

Statement from NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on Governor Sununu
successfully ensuring improvements to New Hampshire mental health
system.

CONCORD - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following
statement following the Executive Council approval for an outside
review of care at New Hampshire Hospital:

"Today’s action by the Executive Council to unanimously approve
an outside review of care at New Hampshire Hospital is yet
another achievement for Governor Sununu in reforming and
redesigning New Hampshire’s mental health system. By spearheading
this review, Governor Sununu is ensuring that patients at our
state psychiatric hospital receive high quality care. Today’s
development continues his legacy of overhauling and improving the
state’s mental health system after years of neglect by Democratic
Governors Shaheen, Lynch and Hassan."

###

name: Republican Party of New Hampshire
subject: NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester Statement on NH Federal Delegation enabling Obamacare fall out
body:
PRESS RELEASE

For Immediate Release: August 2, 2017

Contact: Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310

Statement from NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on New Hampshire's
Democratic Federal Delegation failure to prevent Obamacare
Exchange Increases

CONCORD - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following
statement calling on our Federal Delegation to start working for
New Hampshire families in finding a solution to the massive
increases in the Obamacare Exchange for 2018:

"While New Hampshire families face massive rate increases in the
2018 Obamacare Exchange, Senators Shaheen and Hassan, along with
Congresswomen Kuster and Shea-Porter, are not providing the
leadership that New Hampshire needs in this critical time. The
lack of leadership coming from New Hampshire's delegation in
Washington is extending the burden of the failing Obamacare law
in New Hampshire."

"Voters elect our representatives to go to D.C. to work on
solutions to benefit the state of New Hampshire, and
unfortunately our Federal Delegation has failed to protect New
Hampshire families from a 44% Obamacare premium increase. The
people of New Hampshire need to hold our Federal Delegation
accountable for the fall out of Obamacare and their failure to
enact solutions while the marketplace collapses. New Hampshire
families deserve action, not obstruction, from our Federal
Delegation"

"Jeanne Shaheen and Carol Shea-Porter voted for Obamacare during
the 111th Congress, while Annie Kuster and Maggie Hassan have
failed to propose meaningful reforms to the system. New Hampshire
needs representation that ignores the harsh partisan rhetoric of
D.C. and embraces solutions to build a robust health insurance
market without massive premium increases."

Background:

Union Leader: Obamacare rate hikes confirmed at 44 percent or
higher

clean_body: PRESS RELEASE For Immediate Release: August 2, 2017 Contact: Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310 Statement from NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on New Hampshire's Democratic Federal Delegation failure to prevent Obamacare Exchange Increases CONCORD - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement calling on our Federal Delegation to start working for New Hampshire families in finding a solution to the massive increases in the Obamacare Exchange for 2018: "While New Hampshire families face massive rate increases in the 2018 Obamacare Exchange, Senators Shaheen and Hassan, along with Congresswomen Kuster and Shea-Porter, are not providing the leadership that New Hampshire needs in this critical time. The lack of leadership coming from New Hampshire's delegation in Washington is extending the burden of the failing Obamacare law in New Hampshire." "Voters elect our representatives to go to D.C. to work on solutions to benefit the state of New Hampshire, and unfortunately our Federal Delegation has failed to protect New Hampshire families from a 44% Obamacare premium increase. The people of New Hampshire need to hold our Federal Delegation accountable for the fall out of Obamacare and their failure to enact solutions while the marketplace collapses. New Hampshire families deserve action, not obstruction, from our Federal Delegation" "Jeanne Shaheen and Carol Shea-Porter voted for Obamacare during the 111th Congress, while Annie Kuster and Maggie Hassan have failed to propose meaningful reforms to the system. New Hampshire needs representation that ignores the harsh partisan rhetoric of D.C. and embraces solutions to build a robust health insurance market without massive premium increases." name: Tim Kaine
subject: crossroads
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek,

Virginia is at a crossroads. In one direction we have the extreme ideas of President Trump and his Republican Party. I'm talking about a repeal of the Affordable Care Act, massive tax cuts for the ultra-wealthy, and efforts to gut environmental protections.

In the other direction is economic security for all our families, quality, affordable health care for all our citizens, and a commitment to equality.

That's the direction Ralph Northam will lead us, Derek. **But with the polls in the governor's race as close as they are, it 's going to take every one of us to make sure it's the direction Virginia chooses.**

**[Will you chip in to help my friend Ralph in November?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=JWOVpiriL59W8133%2Fzl5whs4dtJVzGFv)**

I know Ralph is the best choice for this Commonwealth we love. He's proven that he can lead and lead well -- as an Army doctor, as a state legislator, and as our Lieutenant Governor for the past four years.

The entire country is paying attention to this race, and that means outside money is flooding into Virginia for Ralph's opponent. The Republican Governors Association alone is spending $8 million against Ralph, and that's just the tip of the iceberg.

But I know Ralph can win this because he has the people of Virginia behind him. Because Virginians want to build on the McAuliffe-Northam legacy -- not embolden the Trump legacy.

**Are you with me, Derek?[Please let me know by contributing to Ralph's campaign today. Let's fight to elect the governor that Virginia needs and deserves.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=O2NKnYFD02htqbxoKNwuehs4dtJVzGFv)**

Thanks,

Tim

subject: the broken promise of social security >>

body:
[](https://hassan.bsd.net/page/m/f7fded8/2a380d31/1ea2e2bc/fe5abc/112219242/VEsH/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
[](https://hassan.bsd.net/page/m/f7fded8/2a380d31/1ea2e2bc/fe5abc/112219242/VEsE/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
Hello --
Donald Trump wasn't consistent about much during his presidential campaign, but he was steadfast about one thing: if elected, he wouldn't touch Medicare and Social Security.
He's only been in the White House for six months, and Trump's House Republicans are already pushing a budget that would cut $500 billion from these exact programs.
It doesn't stop there, though. Washington Republicans' cruel budget offers tax breaks to billionaires and lines the pockets of corporate special interests, all while leaving some of our most vulnerable friends and neighbors without the promise of Social Security and Medicare.
[**This draconian budget is mean and disrespectful, and all of us on Team Maggie are united in our efforts to stop it from passing. Will you add your name to join us in telling Donald Trump and Paul Ryan not to cut Social Security and Medicare?**](https://hassan.bsd.net/page/m/f7fded8/2a380d31/1ea2e2bc/fe5abd/112219242/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/dontcutsocialsecurity/?code=hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F20170802_socialsecurity")
Thank you for doing whatever it takes to protect these vital programs that allow hard-working Americans to retire with dignity,
Team Maggie
